topics: Introduction: Overview of a database project, its scope and objectives, and the importance of database management systems in real-world applications
Database Design: Conceptual Design using an Entity-Relationship Diagram (ERD) to model entities, relationships, and attributes; and Logical Design to translate the ERD into a relational schema
Database Creation: Implement a sample schema in a DBMS of your choice (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite)
Tables and Relationships: Define tables with appropriate attributes (columns) and establish relationships (foreign keys) between tables
Normalization: Ensure your schema is normalized to minimize redundancy and dependency issues
SQL Queries: Develop a set of SQL queries to demonstrate CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete), complex joins, aggregations, and any specialized queries required by your project
Timeline: Create a timeline or project plan with milestones to ensure timely completion
Constraints: Entity Integrity, Referential Integrity, and Domain Constraints
Transaction Management: Definition of transactions and transaction properties (ACID properties), and implementation of transaction processing logic
Concurrency Control: Techniques to manage concurrent access (e.g., locking, time stamping)
Backup and Recovery: Strategies for database backup and recovery in case of failures
Testing and Validation: Execute queries to validate the correctness of the schema and constraints
Integration with Applications: Develop simple applications (e.g., using Python) to interact with the database
Documentation: Prepare comprehensive documentation covering project objectives, database schema, constraints, transactions, and implementation details
Project Topic Suggestions: Sample project ideas for database management systems
Pharmacy Database Management System
Restaurant Database Management
Payroll Database Management System
Insurance Products Database Management
Hospital Management System
Bank Transactions Management System
